The tricky one is libzfs, which could be fetched with pkgconfig, kmod-zfs-devel does not have this name change, right?

Patch Set 8:

This is strange, works well for me with libzfs2:



   [root@ip-198-19-87-76 ~]# rpm -ql libzfs2-devel | grep pkgconfig
   /usr/share/pkgconfig/libzfs.pc
   /usr/share/pkgconfig/libzfs_core.pc
   [root@ip-198-19-87-76 ~]# rpm -q --provides libzfs2-devel | grep pkgconfig
   pkgconfig(libzfs) = 0.7.13
   pkgconfig(libzfs_core) = 0.7.13
   [root@ip-198-19-87-76 ~]# yum provides 'pkgconfig(libzfs)'
   Loaded plugins: extras_suggestions, langpacks, priorities, update-motd
   libzfs2-devel-0.7.13-0.7.13.122.0.amzn2.x86_64 : Development headers
   Repo        : installed
   Matched from:
   Provides    : pkgconfig(libzfs) = 0.7.13


Could you check 'rpm -q --provides libzfs5-devel | grep pkgconfig' ?

[shaun@cent8 lustre-release]$ rpm -q --provides libzfs5-devel | grep pkgconfig

libnvpair3-devel
libuutil3-devel

libzpool5-devel
[shaun@cent8 lustre-release]$ 

Build was:
./configure --config-cache --with-spec=redhat --with-linux=/usr/src/kernels/4.18.0-305.10.2.ldiskfs.el8.x86_64
make pkg-utils pkg-kmod

Patch Set 8:

That means the libzfs5 packaging has a regression compared to libzfs2 packaging... Could you check that?
I don't have libzfs5 handy, but my old 0.7.13 has correct 'provides'

Patch Set 8:

maybe your libzfs build environment is lacking some pkgconfig package?

Patch Set 8:

> maybe your libzfs build environment is lacking some pkgconfig package?

To verify the build environment I rebuilt zlib locally (and version bump/tagged the package) and dropped in my local repo and used yum update install it.

The end result suggests that the build environment is functional (not the .18-cray tagged package as @System:

[shaun@cent8 ~]$ yum provides 'pkgconfig(zlib)'
Last metadata expiration check: 10:24:26 ago on Wed 13 Apr 2022 12:04:14 PM EDT.
zlib-devel-1.2.11-17.el8.i686 : Header files and libraries for Zlib development
Repo        : baseos
Matched from:
Provide    : pkgconfig(zlib) = 1.2.11

zlib-devel-1.2.11-17.el8.x86_64 : Header files and libraries for Zlib development
Repo        : baseos
Matched from:
Provide    : pkgconfig(zlib) = 1.2.11

zlib-devel-1.2.11-18.cray.el8.x86_64 : Header files and libraries for Zlib development
Repo        : @System
Matched from:
Provide    : pkgconfig(zlib) = 1.2.11

[shaun@cent8 ~]$ 

Looking through the zfs spec changes from 0.7 to current leaves me baffled ...

Patch Set 8:

I suspect something is not quite right with the rpm automatic dependency generation.

Adding an explicit 'Provides: pkgconfig(libzfs)' to the libzfsN-devel package does seem to work:

[shaun@cent8 ~]$ yum provides 'pkgconfig(libzfs)'
Last metadata expiration check: 10:44:02 ago on Wed 13 Apr 2022 12:04:14 PM EDT.
libzfs4-devel-2.0.5-1.el8.x86_64 : Development headers
Repo        : @System
Matched from:
Provide    : pkgconfig(libzfs)

Patch Set 8:

OK, I checked locally libzfs5-devel and can see the same issue. So something changed between zfs2 and zfs5. git-bisect that? 

But using pkgconfig "is the right way to fix it (tm)".

That would avoid the need to any hack to auto detect that.

Code-wise looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-17385 tests: sanity-lfsck 23d fix and enable

commit.subject: LU-13906 build: Conditionally require kmod-zfs-devel
commit.message:
LU-13906 build: Conditionally require kmod-zfs-devel

Server with zfs support requires either kmod-zfs-devel
or a configure that points to the required headers and
library files via configure.

Here we check the configure arguments for '--with-zfs-obj='
if the zfs path is specified for configure the package
requirement is not needed.

Otherwise require the kmod-zfs-devel package and require
one of libzfs-devel, libzfs4-devel or libzfs5-devel

HPE-bug-id: LUS-9743, LUS-10363
Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Change-Id: Ia12239ac7e3912ff50ec7c8e2ceb888862afbc34
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/46356
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Olaf Faaland <faaland1@llnl.gov>
